summary: Ukrainian forces intercepted a Ukrainian Air Force "Hornet" fixed-wing UAV using the "Yolka" anti-drone system, causing its warhead to detonate, while Russian forces reported daily destruction of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and personnel by the "Rubicon" Center, with 90 targets eliminated on April 9 and 173 on April 8. The 147th Separate Artillery Brigade's drone workshop in the Pokrovsk direction conducted continuous UAV repairs, firmware updates, and custom modifications, including 3D printing and battery upgrades. Russian forces used FPV drones to strike two soldiers assaulting Ukrainian positions on motorcycles and killed a Russian soldier who attempted to flee after feigning surrender. Ukrainian soldiers in the Kostiantinivka direction, 36th Marine Brigade, survived an FPV drone attack after detecting it with a drone detector. The "Shuttle" robotic mine clearance system, based on the "Phoenix" all-terrain vehicle, was reported to be deployed in 2024. Company XDOWN unveiled the Small Tactical Unmanned Drone (STUD), a 2.7 kg, 300 km/h FPV drone with a 64 km range and 800 g payload, featuring a Switchblade-style foldable design. Ukrainian forces also used fiber optic-guided FPV drones to destroy Russian 2S22 "Bogdan" self-propelled artillery in the Sumy region.
